Overview

Address
1166 E. 28TH AVENUE, EUGENE, OR 97403
Phone
5413450534
Certified beds
110
Avg daily residents
64 (58% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type

Medicare/Medicaid since
1985-05-17
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 5.51 3.70 5.74 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.34 0.65 0.36 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.43 0.82 1.49 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 3.74 2.23 3.90 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.77 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.04 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
4.93
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.25
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
5.14
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
73.0%
annual
RN turnover
90.0%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.31
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.95
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-08-11 13 11 2 202 1 202
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-03-22 43 33 24 244 2 366
Total weighted health score
243.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (64)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

62 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-12-09 2026-01-05
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-12-09 2026-01-05
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2025-08-11 2025-09-29
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-08-11 2025-09-29
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2025-08-11 2025-09-29
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-08-11 2025-09-29
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2025-08-11 2025-09-29
0628 Provide the required documentation or notification related to the resident's needs, appeal rights, or bed-hold policies. D 2025-08-11 2025-09-29
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-08-11 2025-09-29
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-08-11 2025-09-29
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-08-11 2025-09-29
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. D 2025-08-11 2025-09-29
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. L 2025-08-11 2025-09-29
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-10-16 2024-12-05
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-10-16 2024-12-05
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-10-16 2024-12-05
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2024-10-16 2024-12-05
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-10-16 2024-12-05
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. E 2024-10-16 2024-12-05
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. E 2024-10-16 2024-12-05
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-10-16 2024-12-05
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0576 Ensure residents have reasonable access to and privacy in their use of communication methods. E 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0577 Allow residents to easily view the nursing home's survey results and communicate with advocate agencies. E 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0637 Assess the resident when there is a significant change in condition D 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. E 2024-03-22 2024-06-22
0776 Provide timely, approved x-ray services, or have an agreement with an approved provider to obtain them. D 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. D 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0809 Ensure meals and snacks are served at times in accordance with resident’s needs, preferences, and requests. Suitable and nourishing alternative meals and snacks must be provided for residents who want to eat at non-traditional times or outside of scheduled meal times. D 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. E 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. E 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-03-22 2024-06-22
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. E 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. E 2024-03-22 2024-05-08
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. E 2024-03-22 2024-05-08

Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
